description: Letter from Ṣāliḥ b. Mūsā to Nahray b. Nissim. Dating: ca. 1045 CE. The sender says that Ṣāʿid b. Shuʿayb ordered mats(?) from him, so he had them made, but then Masʿūd married Ṣāʿid's wife(?! Gil suggests divorcee, but maybe fiancee is meant), and now Ṣāʿid doesn't need them any more. The sender now offers the product to Nahray. The sender's brother-in-law Maymūn has sent some bitumen (qifār) with Abū l-Ṭayyib Ḥunayn. He regrets that there are no salt-cured small fish (ṣīr) in his location this year. (Information in part from Gil, Kingdom, Vol. 4, #787.) VMR. ASE.
description_he: מכתב מצאלח בן מוסא לנהראי בן ניסים, כ-1045, על מחצלות שנעשו לצאיד ב. שועיב שכבר לא צריך אותן ועל מוצר חלופי
